source.title: BSEE Accident Investigation Report - Structural Damage Crane Other Lifting

context.region: Gulf of Mexico, West Delta Block 74

event.top_event: Boom section collapse and crush injury
actions_taken: Mechanic Helper used pallet jack to free injured person's leg
Tourniquet applied with 1 inch strap and breaker bar to slow blood loss
Medic assessed injury and applied gauze bandage and splint
Helicopter medical evacuation to Terrebonne General Hospital
event.summary: On 13 May 2022 at approximately 1125 hours, a lead crane mechanic employed by Seatrax was struck and pinned by a falling boom mid-section while removing connector pins from an Aero-G15 crane on platform WD 74 F. The mechanic had struck a connector pin with a sledgehammer to remove it prior to rigging the mid-section to the bridle, contrary to proper procedure. The pin ejected unexpectedly, causing the boom sections to split and fall, pinning the mechanic's right leg between the boom and sledgehammer handle. The mechanic sustained a compound fracture and was evacuated by helicopter to hospital. Root causes included failure to follow proper Job Safety Analysis procedures, inadequate communication between mechanics, and failure to use required Boom Connector Safety Pins that had been left at the hub facility.
key_phrases: Boom Connector Safety Pins not used

hazards.name: Pressurized boom sections with connector pins under load
Suspended load (boom tip section) at height
Manual pin removal using hammer impact

threats.name: Connector pin ejection due to improper removal sequence
Boom section collapse when pin removed without proper rigging support
Unexpected pin ejection on initial hammer strike

controls.name: Job Safety Analysis (JSA) procedure for boom tip changeout
controls.barrier_role: Administrative control to ensure proper work sequence
controls.lod_basis: Procedural control to prevent improper work sequence

supporting_text: The JSA sequence did not cover rigging up of the crane's mid-section to the bridle prior to removing pins
The IP signed the JSA without reading it
No discussion of the proper rigging of the mid-section to bridle prior to removing the Seatrax required boom connector safety pins was included in the JSA

controls.name: Boom Connector Safety Pins (Seatrax required)
controls.barrier_role: Engineering control to prevent boom section separation
controls.lod_basis: Physical restraint to prevent boom sections from separating during pin removal

supporting_text: The Seatrax required boom connecting safety pins were left at the hub
The Boom Connector Safety Pins were left at WD 73 D and no attempt to go back to retrieve them before starting the job was made

controls.name: Rigging mid-section to bridle prior to pin removal
controls.barrier_role: Administrative control to support boom sections during pin removal
controls.lod_basis: Procedural requirement to prevent unsupported boom collapse

supporting_text: Prior to rigging up the mid-section to the bridle, the IP struck the bottom right connector pin with a sledgehammer
No discussion of the proper rigging of the mid-section to bridle prior to removing the Seatrax required boom connector safety pins was included in the JSA

controls.name: Communication and job briefing between mechanics
controls.barrier_role: Administrative control to ensure shared understanding of hazards and procedures
controls.lod_basis: Procedural requirement for job planning and hazard identification

supporting_text: A lack of communication between the two mechanics contributed to the incident
The MH did not cover the proper steps in the JSA or mention the use of the required Boom Connector Safety Pins, and the IP failed to review the JSA
The IP assumed that the MH had covered all the steps

controls.name: First Aid and emergency response
controls.barrier_role: Mitigation control to minimize injury severity
controls.lod_basis: Emergency response to limit consequences of crush injury

supporting_text: The MH immediately radioed the Hub alerting of the incident and requested help
The MH used a nearby pallet jack to free the IP's leg from under the boom
The MH noticed blood from the IP's leg and applied a tourniquet with a 1 inch strap and breaker bar to slow the blood loss
First Aid was given, and the IP was transported by helicopter to a local hospital

controls.name: Medical evacuation by helicopter
controls.barrier_role: Mitigation control to provide rapid access to definitive medical care
controls.lod_basis: Emergency response to minimize injury consequences

supporting_text: At approximately 1142 hours, a helicopter that was in the area and contracted by Cox, was directed to land at WD 74 F for medical evacuation
Flight time from platform to hospital was approximately 30 minutes
The IP was stabilized and given medication by medical staff

notes.rules: JSON output only. mentioned fields must be evidence-based. Use null for unknown values.
